pi_items.source: Calculated by converting the target latitude 35.215 degrees explicitly provided in the task description and setup_task.sh to radians (35.215 * pi / 180 ≈ 0.6146).
Calculated by converting the target longitude -111.633 degrees explicitly provided in the task description and setup_task.sh to radians (-111.633 * pi / 180 ≈ -1.9484).

Derived from explicit task instructions: 'Enable constellation lines' (flag_constellation_drawing=True), 'enable constellation labels/names' (flag_constellation_name=True), 'disable the ground/landscape' (flag_landscape=False), and 'enable the atmosphere' (flag_atmosphere=True).
Derived from the task instructions which mandate documenting the Perseid event, locating the Moon, and identifying the radiant constellation (Perseus) in the output text file.

privileged_info_summary: The required observation location coordinates exactly match the expected rad values of 0.6146 N and -1.9484 W, corresponding to the 35.215 N, -111.633 W location provided dynamically via a synthetic script (meteor_showers.json). The display flags metadata accurately reflects the text instructions to turn on constellation lines and names, disable the landscape, and enable the atmosphere. The required keywords list accurately reflects the objects (Perseus, Moon) and event (Perseid) mandated by the prompt.
